QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_24

question.text: Under the NPS investment guidelines, what is the maximum exposure to any single industry?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The maximum exposure to any single industry is capped at 15% of the portfolio. This ensures diversification across sectors. **The Trap:** This is an investment risk management guideline, not an asset class allocation limit. Industry exposure and asset class limits are different. **Cross-Exam Context:** NPS investment guidelines also prohibit investment outside India. Money market instruments are limited to 10% with minimum A1+ rating. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Explain industry diversification limits to clients as a risk management feature. No single sector can dominate their NPS portfolio.
